title: How a Jet Engine Turns Air Into Thrust
subject: jet_engine

steps.title: Air Enters The Fan
steps.narration: The front fan pulls a large stream of air into the engine. Some air bypasses the core, and the rest moves into the compressor.

steps.title: The Compressor Raises Pressure
steps.narration: Rows of spinning compressor blades squeeze the incoming air. Higher pressure makes the next combustion step far more energetic.

steps.title: Fuel Burns In The Combustor
steps.narration: Fuel mixes with the compressed air and burns. The hot expanding gas now carries much more energy downstream.

steps.title: The Turbine Powers The Compressor
steps.narration: Hot gas spins the turbine. The turbine is connected to the compressor by the central shaft, so it pays back the work needed to keep compressing air.

steps.title: Fast Exhaust Makes Thrust
steps.narration: The remaining hot gas accelerates through the nozzle. Pushing that mass of air backward creates forward thrust.

quiz.question: What connects the turbine's work back to the compressor?
quiz.wrongDiagnosis: You missed that the turbine drives the compressor through the central shaft.

sources.title: NASA Glenn Research Center: Jet Engine
sources.summary: NASA explains how turbine engines compress air, add fuel and heat, extract work in the turbine, and accelerate exhaust for thrust.

sources.title: FAA Pilot's Handbook: Turbine Engines
sources.summary: FAA training material describes compressor, combustor, turbine, and exhaust sections in aircraft turbine engines.
